mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness signals accelerating wage growth and consumer spending power, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives immediate positive equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and industrial production confirms broad-based economic expansion rather than inventory-driven supply shocks. This dual confirmation validates earnings growth sustainability and lifts equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a GDPC1-only baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward tests, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, rising core inflation combined with stable short-term rates signals that the Federal Reserve is allowing the economy to normalize without premature tightening. This environment supports equity risk premiums and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5 percent while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent, indicating that inflation expectations are unanchoring and triggering a hawkish policy pivot.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts directly reflect builder confidence and household demand for durable goods. This leading indicator reliably precedes positive construction spending and related consumer outlays, driving short-term equity outperformance.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month changes show zero correlation with 5-day SPX returns across all available data, or the signal generates losses during housing booms due to rate hike expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 200,000 jobs confirms robust labor demand and household income growth. This threshold breach validates economic momentum, reducing recession fears and triggering positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are flat or negative in more than 60% of months where PAYEMS exceeds 200,000, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when conditioned on UNRATE movements.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with falling unemployment indicate a virtuous cycle of income-driven consumption. This interaction confirms sustainable demand expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and lifting equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 3 percent but UNRATE remains above 6.0 percent, indicating that consumption is debt-fueled and unsustainable.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, industrial production growth outpacing GDP growth signals supply-side efficiency gains and capacity utilization improvements. This dynamic compresses input costs and boosts profit margins, driving immediate equity gains.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0 percent but GDPC1 growth falls below 0.5 percent, indicating supply gluts that trigger deflationary fears and margin compression.
